codeReview.summary: Core auth/session endpoints and middleware are implemented with strong test coverage, but two security requirements in this feature's fulfillment set are not met.

issues.description: SESSION_SECRET falls back to a hardcoded default (`process.env.SESSION_SECRET ?? 'default-dev-secret-key-min-32-chars!!'`). This violates VAL-SEC-003 (session secret must come from environment with no hardcoded secret).

Login returns immediately for non-existent users and only runs `Bun.password.verify` when a user exists, creating measurable timing differences between invalid-username and invalid-password paths. This does not satisfy VAL-SEC-002 timing-consistency expectations.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `fullstack-worker` procedure assumes UI/manual browser verification as a standard step, but this feature was API-only. The worker feedback explicitly requested an API-only exception, suggesting the skill procedure should better distinguish API-only features from full UI flows.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/fullstack-worker/SKILL.md step 8 requires agent-browser manual verification; handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges for session d7ce6a37-9e80-48cc-94fe-5d9ea7014441 notes this mismatch.

summary: Reviewed feature auth-api-session-middleware (commit 67d9a52) using handoff, transcript skeleton, and diff. Implementation is functionally substantial, but it fails security acceptance due to a hardcoded SESSION_SECRET fallback and login timing-path inconsistency.
